---
title: "MP011: Principles System Architecture"
subtitle: "Complete three-part documentation structure of the 00_principles system"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "MP"
number: "011"
date-created: "2025-04-01"
date-modified: "2025-08-25"
author: "Claude"
type: "meta-principle"
derives_from:
  - "MP001: Axiomatization System"
  - "MP034: Archive Immutability"
influences:
  - "MP002: Primitive Terms and Definitions"
  - "MP003: Structural Blueprint"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Core Concept {#core-concept}

The 00_principles documentation system follows a structured, hierarchical organization with `natural/` as the primary source of truth. The system is organized into THREE distinct parts: theoretical foundations (part1_principles), practical implementations (part2_implementations), and domain-specific knowledge (part3_domain_knowledge). All documentation is maintained in Quarto Markdown (.qmd) format within a dual-language (en/zh) structure, while root-level .md files are considered deprecated legacy artifacts.

:::{.callout-important}
## Primary Documentation System
The `natural/` directory contains the ONLY official and authoritative documentation system, organized into three complementary parts:
1. **Part 1: Principles** - Theoretical foundations (MP, P, R)
2. **Part 2: Implementations** - Practical patterns, tools, and templates
3. **Part 3: Domain Knowledge** - Specialized domain-specific applications
:::

# Directory Architecture {#architecture}

## Canonical Structure {#canonical-structure}

The 00_principles directory follows this authoritative three-part structure:

```
00_principles/
├── natural/                    # PRIMARY: Official documentation system
│   ├── en/                    # English language version
│   │   ├── part1_principles/  # PART 1: Theoretical foundations
│   │   │   ├── CH00_fundamental_principles/
│   │   │   │   ├── 01_general_principles/
│   │   │   │   ├── 02_structure_organization/
│   │   │   │   ├── 03_development_methodology/
│   │   │   │   ├── 04_data_management/
│   │   │   │   ├── 05_terminology_standards/
│   │   │   │   └── 06_languages/
│   │   │   ├── CH01_structure_organization/
│   │   │   ├── CH02_data_management/
│   │   │   ├── CH03_development_methodology/
│   │   │   ├── CH04_ui_components/
│   │   │   ├── CH05_testing_deployment/
│   │   │   └── CH06_integration_collaboration/
│   │   ├── part2_implementations/  # PART 2: Practical implementations
│   │   │   ├── CH03_structure_rules/     # Additional structural rules
│   │   │   ├── CH07_solutions_patterns/  # Solution patterns (S, SLN)
│   │   │   ├── CH08_data_processing/     # Data frameworks (DF, ETL)
│   │   │   ├── CH09_modules_tools/       # Modules (M)
│   │   │   ├── CH10_templates_examples/  # Templates and examples
│   │   │   ├── CH11_app_construction/    # Application patterns
│   │   │   ├── CH12_derivations/         # Derivations (D)
│   │   │   ├── CH13_connections/         # Connections (C)
│   │   │   ├── CH14_functions_reference/ # Function documentation
│   │   │   └── appendix/                 # Tools and utilities
│   │   └── part3_domain_knowledge/ # PART 3: Specialized domains
│   │       └── CH20_marketing_analytics/ # Marketing-specific knowledge
│   └── zh/                    # Chinese language version (mirrors en/)
│       ├── part1_principles/  # 第一部分：理論基礎
│       ├── part2_implementations/ # 第二部分：實際實作
│       └── part3_domain_knowledge/ # 第三部分：領域知識
├── CHANGELOG/                  # Version history and archived content
│   ├── archive/               # Historical versions (IMMUTABLE per MP034)
│   ├── decisions/             # Architecture decision records
│   └── releases/              # Release notes
├── INDEX.md                   # Root index (deprecated, for reference only)
├── README.md                  # Root readme (deprecated, for reference only)
└── *.md                       # Any root .md files are DEPRECATED/LEGACY
```

:::{.callout-warning}
## Critical Requirements
1. **ALL documentation MUST be organized within the three-part structure**
2. **Part 1 contains formal principles (MP, P, R)**
3. **Part 2 contains implementation guidance (M, S, D, C, DF, ETL, etc.)**
4. **Part 3 contains domain-specific knowledge**
5. **ALL documentation MUST use .qmd format (Quarto Markdown)**
6. **Root-level .md files are NOT official and should be ignored**
7. **Archive directories are IMMUTABLE per MP034**
:::

## File Format Requirements {#file-format}

### Official Format: Quarto Markdown (.qmd)

All principles must use the .qmd extension with proper YAML frontmatter:

:::{.panel-tabset}

### ✅ Correct (Official .qmd)
```yaml
---
title: "MP011: Documentation Organization"
subtitle: "Meta-principle for documentation structure"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "MP"
number: "011"
date-created: "2025-04-01"
date-modified: "2025-08-25"
type: "meta-principle"
---
```

### ❌ Incorrect (Legacy .md)
```markdown
# MP011: Documentation Organization

Legacy .md format - NOT OFFICIAL
Should not be referenced or maintained
```

:::

## Three-Part Division Rationale {#three-part-rationale}

The documentation system is intentionally divided into three complementary parts, each serving a distinct purpose:

:::{.callout-tip}
## The Three Parts Explained

### Part 1: Principles (Theoretical Foundations)
- **Purpose**: Establishes the theoretical and architectural foundations
- **Content**: Meta-Principles (MP), Principles (P), and Rules (R)
- **Chapters**: CH00-CH06 covering fundamental concepts through integration
- **Nature**: Prescriptive, defining what the system IS and HOW it should be built

### Part 2: Implementations (Practical Patterns)
- **Purpose**: Provides practical implementation guidance and reusable components
- **Content**: Solutions (S/SLN), Modules (M), Derivations (D), Connections (C), Data Frameworks (DF/ETL), Templates, Examples
- **Chapters**: CH03, CH07-CH14, appendix
- **Nature**: Demonstrative, showing HOW to apply the principles in practice

### Part 3: Domain Knowledge (Specialized Applications)
- **Purpose**: Contains domain-specific knowledge and specialized applications
- **Content**: Domain-specific frameworks, methodologies, and calculations
- **Chapters**: CH13 (marketing analytics), extensible for other domains
- **Nature**: Applied, addressing specific business or technical domains
:::

This three-part structure creates a clear separation between:
1. **Theory** (what we believe and why)
2. **Practice** (how we implement it)
3. **Application** (where we apply it to specific domains)

## Language Organization {#language-organization}

The system maintains parallel structures for English and Chinese across all three parts:

| Path Component | English | Chinese | Purpose |
|---------------|---------|---------|---------|
| Language root | `natural/en/` | `natural/zh/` | Language separation |
| Part 1 | `part1_principles/` | `part1_principles/` | Theoretical foundations |
| Part 2 | `part2_implementations/` | `part2_implementations/` | Practical patterns |
| Part 3 | `part3_domain_knowledge/` | `part3_domain_knowledge/` | Domain applications |
| Chapters | `CH00-CH14` | `CH00-CH14` | Topic organization |
| Files | `*.qmd` | `*.qmd` | Individual documents |

:::{.callout-note}
## Dual-Language Synchronization
Both language versions must maintain identical structure and numbering across all three parts. Changes to one language should be reflected in the other to maintain consistency.
:::

# Chapter Organization {#chapters}

## Chapter Structure Across Three Parts {#chapter-structure}

The documentation is organized into logical chapters distributed across the three parts:

### Part 1: Principles (CH00-CH06)

| Chapter | Topic | Content Types |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| **CH00** | Fundamental Principles | Meta-principles (MP) establishing system foundations |
| **CH01** | Structure & Organization | Principles (P) and Rules (R) for code organization |
| **CH02** | Data Management | Data handling principles and rules |
| **CH03** | Development Methodology | Development practices and standards |
| **CH04** | UI Components | User interface principles and patterns |
| **CH05** | Testing & Deployment | Testing strategies and deployment rules |
| **CH06** | Integration & Collaboration | System integration patterns |

### Part 2: Implementations (CH03, CH07-CH14, appendix)

| Chapter | Topic | Content Types |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| **CH03** | Structure Rules | Additional structural rules beyond Part 1 |
| **CH07** | Solutions & Patterns | Solution patterns (S, SLN) for common problems |
| **CH08** | Data Processing | Data frameworks (DF) and ETL pipelines |
| **CH09** | Modules & Tools | Reusable modules (M) and utilities |
| **CH10** | Templates & Examples | Code templates, examples, and guides |
| **CH11** | App Construction | Application building patterns |
| **CH12** | Derivations | Derived implementations (D) |
| **CH13** | Connections | System connections (C) |
| **CH14** | Functions Reference | Comprehensive function documentation |
| **appendix** | Tools & Utilities | Development tools and validation utilities |

### Part 3: Domain Knowledge (Currently CH13)

| Chapter | Topic | Content Types |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| **CH13** | Marketing Analytics | IPT framework, calculation methodologies |
| **(extensible)** | Other Domains | Future domain-specific content |

## Subcategory Organization {#subcategories}

Within CH00_fundamental_principles, subcategories provide further organization:

```
CH00_fundamental_principles/
├── 01_general_principles/      # Core axioms and foundations
├── 02_structure_organization/  # System structure (includes THIS principle)
├── 03_development_methodology/  # Development approaches
├── 04_data_management/         # Data principles
├── 05_terminology_standards/   # Naming and terminology
└── 06_languages/               # NSQL and specialized languages
```

# Document Classification {#classification}

## Content Type System {#content-types}

The documentation system uses specific prefixes to classify different types of content:

### Part 1: Formal Principles

| Type | Prefix | Location | Purpose |
|------|--------|----------|---------|
| **Meta-Principles** | MP | CH00 only | System foundations and axioms |
| **Principles** | P | CH01-CH06 | Implementation guidelines |
| **Rules** | R | CH01-CH06 | Specific technical requirements |

### Part 2: Implementation Patterns

| Type | Prefix | Location | Purpose |
|------|--------|----------|---------|
| **Solutions** | S, SLN | CH07 | Problem-solving patterns |
| **Modules** | M | CH09 | Reusable functional modules |
| **Derivations** | D | CH12 | Derived implementations |
| **Connections** | C | CH13 | System integration patterns |
| **Data Frameworks** | DF | CH08 | Data pipeline architectures |
| **ETL Patterns** | ETL | CH08 | Extract-Transform-Load patterns |
| **Examples** | EX | CH10 | Code examples |
| **Guides** | GUIDE | CH10 | Implementation guides |
| **Templates** | RC, T | CH10 | Reusable code templates |
| **Functions** | FN | CH14 | Function documentation |
| **Tools** | TOOL | appendix | Development utilities |

### Part 3: Domain Knowledge

| Type | Prefix | Location | Purpose |
|------|--------|----------|---------|
| **Marketing** | MK | CH13 | Marketing-specific frameworks |
| **(extensible)** | - | - | Future domain prefixes |

:::{.callout-tip}
## Classification Logic
- **Part 1 (MP, P, R)**: Defines what the system IS and establishes rules
- **Part 2 (M, S, D, C, etc.)**: Shows HOW to implement the principles
- **Part 3 (MK, etc.)**: Applies principles to specific domains
:::

# Archive Management {#archives}

## MP034: Archive Immutability {#archive-immutability}

Per MP034, all content in `CHANGELOG/archive/` is permanently immutable:

:::{.callout-critical}
## Absolute Archive Protection
- **NEVER** modify files in any `archive/` directory
- **NEVER** rename or reorganize archived content
- **NEVER** include archived files in bulk operations
- Archives serve as permanent historical records
:::

## Archive Structure {#archive-structure}

```
CHANGELOG/
├── archive/                    # All archived content (IMMUTABLE)
│   ├── principles_processed/   # Legacy P and R files
│   ├── principles_converted_*/ # Historical conversions
│   └── CH00_fundamental_*/     # Previous organizations
├── decisions/                  # Architecture decision records
└── releases/                   # Version history
```

# Quarto Integration {#quarto}

## Build System {#build-system}

The documentation uses Quarto for cross-referencing and building:

```yaml
# _quarto.yml configuration
project:
  type: book
  output-dir: _book

book:
  title: "Principles Documentation"
  chapters:
    - index.qmd
    - part: "Part I: Principles"
      chapters:
        - natural/en/part1_principles/CH00*/index.qmd
        - natural/en/part1_principles/CH01*/index.qmd
```

## Cross-References {#cross-references}

Quarto enables rich cross-references between principles:

```markdown
See [MP001: Axiomatization System](../01_general_principles/MP001_axiomatization_system.qmd)
for foundational concepts.
```

# Migration Path {#migration}

## From Legacy to Current {#legacy-migration}

For systems still referencing root-level .md files:

1. **Identify**: Find all references to root .md files
2. **Map**: Locate corresponding .qmd in `natural/*/part1_principles/`
3. **Update**: Change references to point to official .qmd files
4. **Verify**: Ensure all links and references work correctly

:::{.panel-tabset}

### ❌ Old Reference (Deprecated)
```r
source("00_principles/MP001_axiomatization.md")
```

### ✅ New Reference (Official)
```r
# Reference the official .qmd location
principle_path <- file.path(
  "00_principles",
  "natural", 
  "en",
  "part1_principles",
  "CH00_fundamental_principles",
  "01_general_principles",
  "MP001_axiomatization_system.qmd"
)
```

:::

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## Documentation Compliance Checklist

When working with principles documentation:

- [ ] Place all new principles in `natural/*/part1_principles/` structure
- [ ] Use .qmd format exclusively for principles
- [ ] Include proper YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Maintain dual-language synchronization (en/zh)
- [ ] Follow chapter organization (CH00-CH14)
- [ ] Respect MP034 archive immutability
- [ ] Update both language versions for changes
- [ ] Use Quarto cross-references for linking
- [ ] Ignore root-level .md files (deprecated)
- [ ] Never modify anything in `archive/` directories
:::

# Benefits {#benefits}

:::{.callout-tip}
## Advantages of This Architecture

1. **Clear Authority**: Single source of truth in `natural/` directory
2. **Language Support**: Native dual-language organization
3. **Rich Documentation**: Quarto enables advanced features
4. **Historical Preservation**: Archives maintain complete history
5. **Logical Organization**: Chapter-based structure aids discovery
6. **Type Safety**: .qmd format ensures consistent structure
7. **Build Integration**: Quarto enables book-style documentation
8. **Cross-Referencing**: Internal links and references work reliably
:::

# Relationships with Other Principles {#relationships}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Documentation Organization Dependencies"
%%| fig-align: "center"

graph TD
    MP011[MP011: Documentation Organization]
    MP001[MP001: Axiomatization System]
    MP034[MP034: Archive Immutability]
    MP003[MP003: Structural Blueprint]
    
    MP001 -->|defines classification| MP011
    MP034 -->|protects archives| MP011
    MP011 -->|organizes within| MP003
    
    style MP011 fill:#ffd700,stroke:#333,stroke-width:3px
    style MP034 fill:#ff6b6b,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
```

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

This meta-principle establishes the authoritative documentation architecture for the entire 00_principles system. By maintaining all official documentation in `natural/*/part1_principles/` using .qmd format, we ensure consistency, discoverability, and maintainability while preserving historical records through immutable archives.

:::{.callout-important}
## Key Takeaway
The `natural/` directory in .qmd format is the ONLY official principles documentation. Root .md files are deprecated legacy artifacts. This architecture ensures a single source of truth with rich documentation capabilities.
:::
